author: kanewallmann
content:
Rocket Pool relies on state proofs for proving Validator state in BeaconState and for proving a Withdrawal in the ExecutionPayload withdrawals list.

We are not dependent on the availability of every single block root in the beacon roots contract. For Validator proofs, any block root suffices as the Validator list is always available. For Withdrawal proofs, we prove this via an indirect path already, either through block_roots or historical_summaries. Because of this indirection, if the block root for the exact slot the withdrawal occurred is not available, we can use any block root that follows.

There would likely be some minor client work required in the slot selection logic during proof generation to avoid selecting a slot which doesn't have it's block root available in the EVM. But on the contract side, there is no impact to us whichever option is selected.

In your summary, you mention "+22 hases static by proving through the state/parent_roots list". Did you mean to say historical_summaries here? (has a length of 2^24 not 2^22) 

TL;DR Rocket Pool already handles this application-side so is mostly unaffected by this decision.

author: kanewallmann
content: Disregard comment about historical_summaries, I see you meant through block_roots (3 to state_root, 6 to block_roots, then 13 through the list, etc.)
